## 3. Deep Research panel

**Lifetime DR verdicts (n=70 of 384 = 18.2% DR coverage overall):**

| Verdict | Count | Share of DR runs |
|---|---|---|
| ADJUSTED | 39 | 55.7% |
| OVERRIDDEN | 25 | 35.7% |
| ERROR_PARSING | 3 | 4.3% |
| UPGRADED | 1 | 1.4% |
| INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS | 1 | 1.4% |
| CONFIRMED | 1 | 1.4% |

**DR action vs. PM rec (lifetime, only DR-reviewed rows):**

| PM rec | → AVOID | → WATCH | → BUY_LIMIT | → BUY |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BUY (n=48 DR'd) | 20 (42%) | 1 | 20 | 7 |
| BUY_LIMIT (n=21 DR'd) | 8 (38%) | 0 | 12 | 1 |

DR kills **~40% of PM-greenlit ideas** outright — a brutal but disciplined senior-reviewer layer. ERROR_PARSING rate (4.3%) is low but persistent and crosses several model versions (HQY, PPERF, ADBE — all historical).

**Today's individual DR cases:**

| ID | Ticker | PM | DR verdict | DR action | Conviction | Reasoning excerpt |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 380 | GAP | BUY_LIMIT | **OVERRIDDEN** | **AVOID** | HIGH | "Council mistook a fully priced-in, non-operating $313M legal settlement and an authorized buyback as fundamental catalysts, entirely ignoring the structural margin destruction from tariffs and Athleta's collapse." |

Only **1 of 9** today's candidates received DR (and only 1 of 2 bullish PM verdicts — FLUT BUY_LIMIT did NOT get DR'd). Today's DR throughput is below historical pace; see Section 6 for severity.

---

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **The DR override layer is working.** 25 lifetime OVERRIDDEN verdicts (35.7% of DR runs), and today's GAP HIGH-conviction kill is a textbook save — surfaced exactly the kind of "fully-priced-in catalyst" misread that the council is structurally prone to. Keep it.
- **The DR layer is also bypassed too often.** 38 bullish PM rows lifetime never saw DR. That's roughly 35% of all BUY/BUY_LIMIT verdicts going straight to "Owned" status with no senior review. If GAP today had been one of those, the desk would have entered a long.
- **Closed book is healthy but thin.** +42 pp realized over 36 closes ≈ +1.17 pp/trade. Win-rate is barely above 50% but TPs are larger than SLs (good asymmetry). The top 10 winners contribute 80%+ of the realized P&L — heavily concentrated in the early-April cohort (TEAM, CHRD, GWRE, NICE, PANW, NOW, IOT, OKTA — all from the 4/9–4/18 window).
- **The May cohort is weaker than the April cohort.** May closes are mostly small DESK_DECISION exits or STOP_LOSS hits (CE −9.15, ALC −6.74, NICE −8.74); April closes were dominated by TP hits. Possible regime shift or alpha decay; needs evaluation when `decision_tracking` is restored.
- **`decision_tracking` being empty is the single biggest gap in the whole audit picture.** Without it, no backtesting harness can be built (item #1 in CLAUDE.md's improvement backlog). Everything we say about "did the recommendation work" is implicit in desk_positions, which only sees the 9% of recs that became live trades.
- **WTW is the most urgent operational call.** 1.15% to stop on a position the desk hasn't reviewed today is asking to get knocked out on a routine wiggle.
- **Today's macro-selloff cluster (DG/TGT/ETSY/U) all hitting -5% to -6% within 90 minutes** suggests a real index-level event, not idiosyncratic news. PM correctly stayed defensive (4 AVOID + 1 WATCH on the four), and gatekeeper correctly let them through DEEP_DIP/STANDARD_DIP tiers. Pipeline behavior here looks right.
- **Small-sample caveat:** today's "0 net new tickets" is noise — pipeline is still warm with only 9 candidates so far this session, vs. 14-day mean of 16. Wait one more session before treating low throughput as a trend.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions (prioritised)

1. **🔴 Wire up the audit destinations.** Add `/Users/simonbaumgart/Desktop/private/Stocks/Investment Decisions Claude/` and `/Users/simonbaumgart/Documents/Claude/Projects/Investment Ideas and Portfolio/stockdrop/` to this scheduled task's connected folders so reports actually land where you read them. Today (and 5/8) both fell through to `audit_reports/`.
2. **🔴 Triage WTW stop tonight.** 1.15% headroom is too thin to leave unattended. Either widen the stop, tighten the desk review cadence, or close the position.
3. **🔴 Restore `decision_tracking` writes.** Whatever cron or service is supposed to be polling and inserting post-decision price points has stopped. This is blocking the entire backtesting/feedback agenda from CLAUDE.md.
4. **🔴 Investigate the BUY/BUY_LIMIT → no-DR gap.** Determine whether (a) DR is rate-limited and silently dropping items, (b) the deep_research queue is leaking, or (c) some PM verdicts route around DR entirely. Today's FLUT is the live test case.
5. **🟠 Re-run desk position review.** All 6 ACTIVE positions are showing Saturday's marks; desk-review cron appears to have skipped 5/11. Re-run before tomorrow's open.
6. **🟠 Decide `ai_score`'s fate.** If deprecated, drop it from the schema and dashboard; if active, fix the writer. 97% NULL is worse than not having the column.
7. **🟠 Reconcile `decision_points.status` with `desk_positions`.** Write a one-shot script to flip stale "Owned" rows whose desk_position is CLOSED (15 rows), and audit/decide the other ~79 orphaned-Owned rows.
8. **🟠 Purge test rows from `decision_points`.** 9 rows from 2026-05-09 (TEST*, T8_*) are skewing counts. Either soft-delete or move to a fixtures/dev table.
9. **🟡 Audit PENDING > 24h backlog.** 18 real symbols (APA, PBMRF, TPL, DOCN, …) stuck without final verdict for days/weeks. Either re-run, mark as ABANDONED, or expire.
10. **🟡 Harden the DR parser** for the formats that produced HQY/PPERF/ADBE ERROR_PARSING. 4.3% error rate is recoverable.
11. **🟡 Track NULL `git_version`** — all 9 of today's rows lack it. If today's deploy stopped emitting the tag, that's a release-pipeline regression worth a 10-minute look.
